search:kubernetes-metrics-server:kuberne.content:
- **Metrics Server Installation**:
  - Use `kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/metrics-server/releases/latest/download/components.yaml` to install the latest version.

- **Key Config Options**:
  - `--kubelet-insecure-tls`: Allows metrics server to connect to kubelets without TLS.
  - `--cert-dir`: Specifies the directory where certificates are stored.
  - `--kubelet-preferred-address-types`: Defines the preferred address types for kubelet connections.

- **Optimizing Metrics Server**:
  - Increase `--request-header-client-ca-file` size if experiencing high request sizes.
  - Adjust `--secure-port` to use a non-standard port for secure communication.

- **CLI Commands**:
  - `kubectl get pods --all-namespaces | grep metrics-server`: Lists all metrics server pods across namespaces.
  - `kubectl logs <metrics-server-pod-name> -n kube-system`: Fetches logs from the metrics server pod.

- **Latest Version Check**:
  - Visit [Metrics Server GitHub Releases](https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/metrics-server/releases) for the latest version details.

- **CLI Commands**:
  - `kubectl top nodes`: Displays resource usage of nodes.
  - `kubectl top pods --all-namespaces`: Shows resource usage for all pods across namespaces.

- **Latest Versions**:
  - Kubernetes Metrics Server: v0.6.3 (as of Jan 2023)

- **Key Config Options**:
  - `--kubelet-preferred-address-types=InternalIP,ExternalIP,Hostname`: Specifies the preferred address types for kubelets.
  - `--metric-resolution=1m`: Sets the resolution at which metrics are collected and exposed.

search:kubernetes-metrics-server:metrics.content:
- **CLI Commands**:
  - `kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/metrics-server/releases/latest/download/components.yaml`
  - `kubectl get pods --all-namespaces | grep metrics-server`

- **Latest Versions**:
  - Metrics Server: v0.8.0

- **Key Config Options**:
  - `--kubelet-insecure-tls=true` (required for versions > 0.7.1)
  - `--cert-dir=/tmp` (optional, specify certificate directory)
